title: hex docs — Static Site Generator

behaviors.description: hex docs generate produces a self-contained static site
behaviors.given: hex docs generate is run from a hex project root
then: A directory hex-docs/ (or --output path) is created
index.html exists and references no external URLs
All CSS is bundled in static/style.css
All pages are valid HTML5
No page requires a running server to render

behaviors.description: Commands section enumerates all top-level commands and subcommands
behaviors.given: the binary's clap Command tree is walked at generation time
behaviors.when: commands/index.html and per-command pages are generated
then: Every top-level command from hex --help appears as a page
Each page lists all subcommands with their descriptions
Each page lists all flags with their types, defaults, and descriptions
Short and long forms of flags are both shown
Required vs optional flags are distinguished

behaviors.description: Skills section is generated from embedded skill frontmatter
behaviors.given: hex-cli/assets/skills/ contains SKILL.md files with YAML frontmatter
behaviors.when: skills/index.html and per-skill pages are generated
then: All 20 hex-native skills appear in the index
Each skill page shows: name, trigger, description, full body content
The trigger is displayed as a copyable slash command (e.g. /hex-scaffold)
Skills are sorted alphabetically

behaviors.description: Agents section is generated from embedded agent YAMLs
behaviors.when: agents/index.html and per-agent pages are generated
then: All 14 agent roles appear with their name and description
Each agent page shows the full YAML source in a code block
Model tier, workflow phases, and quality thresholds are highlighted

behaviors.description: ADRs section renders all decision records
then: ADR index groups records by status: Accepted, Proposed, Deprecated
Each ADR is rendered from markdown to HTML via pulldown-cmark
ADR frontmatter (Status, Date) is shown in a metadata block
Internal ADR cross-references (ADR-XXX) become hyperlinks

behaviors.description: Client-side search works without a server
behaviors.given: the generated site is opened in a browser from the filesystem
behaviors.when: a user types in the search box
then: Results appear from commands, skills, agents, and ADRs
Search is against a pre-built JSON index bundled at generation time
No network request is made

behaviors.given: any page in the generated site
then: hex version is shown (e.g. hex 26.4.0)
Generation timestamp is shown (ISO 8601)
A link to regenerate docs is shown (hex docs generate)

behaviors.description: hex docs serve opens docs via hex-nexus
then: hex-nexus serves the generated site at /docs
The default browser opens http://localhost:5555/docs
If hex-docs/ does not exist, generate is run automatically first
All output is written to the specified path
The path is created if it does not exist
A summary of pages generated is printed to stdout

non_goals: Authentication or access control on docs
Hosting infrastructure (the command generates files only)
Versioned docs for multiple hex versions simultaneously
acceptance_criteria: hex docs generate completes in under 5 seconds on a cold run
Generated site passes HTML5 validation (no structural errors)
All 20 hex-native skills appear in skills/index.html
ADR index shows correct status groupings
No page references external URLs (fully offline-capable)
